home.title: Gui Beltrame - Build solos from scratch
home.description: Learn to build solos with personality using the technique you already have. A structured method for guitar players who want to break out of autopilot and improvise with freedom.
home.ogTitle: Gui Beltrame - Build solos from scratch
home.ogDescription: A structured method for guitar players who want to break out of autopilot and improvise with creative freedom.

heading.prefix: Have you ever found yourself in

pain.subheading: If at least one of these lines hits home, know that there's a way to change it.

1.title: Your solos always sound the same
1.description: You go up and down the same scale, repeat the same clichés, and feel like every solo sounds identical.
1.imageAlt: Illustrates the feeling of being stuck on the same licks and phrases on guitar

2.title: Insecurity when improvising
2.description: On stage or in your bedroom, you feel insecure improvising because you don't own the fretboard.
2.imageAlt: Represents the creative block when it's time to improvise on guitar

3.title: Tons of content, little progress
3.description: You get lost in the flood of online content and don't even know where to start to create and improvise.
3.imageAlt: Suggests the gap between heavy studying and little real-world music application

4.title: You compare yourself and get frustrated
4.description: You get frustrated watching other guitarists create with the same scales you know and ask yourself: "why can't I do that?"
4.imageAlt: Captures the frustration of comparing your own sound to other guitar players

5.title: You play without understanding what you do
5.description: You can only play what you've memorized and don't truly understand any of it — you're just running on autopilot.
5.imageAlt: Visualizes the lack of direction and roadmap when studying guitar

6.title: You feel stuck
6.description: You've been studying for a while, but feel stagnant and discouraged because you don't see progress and your solos keep sounding the same.
6.imageAlt: Speaks to the search for a personal sound and musical identity on guitar

heading.highlight: was never a lack of talent.

reframe.p1Before: I get it — most people have already spent hours and hours studying, just like I did. It's frustrating to keep stacking up content and feel like there's
reframe.p2Before: And then comes the feeling that you're just an
reframe.p2After: with nothing that sets you apart.
reframe.p3Before: That's not true. Every guitar player has
reframe.p3Strong: unique traits that can be developed
reframe.p3After: — but you can't rely on inspiration alone.
reframe.manifestoLine1: No scale or technique can save
reframe.p5Before: Without structure, your improvisation turns into the classic "up-and-down" scale run. And your improvising can't depend solely on a good day of
reframe.quote1: But what if I told you there's a way to develop your own language and not depend only on inspiration to create your own solos — using a CREATIVE VISUALIZATION SYSTEM?
reframe.quote2: But first, let me introduce myself...

authority.videoTitle: Studio recording of the original band's album (Vimeo)
authority.videoCaption: Studio recording of my original band's album
authority.heading: I'M GUI BELTRAME
authority.p1: I'm a teacher and the creator of the CREATIVE VISUALIZATION SYSTEM. For years, I dealt with the exact same problems you're dealing with. And there was a feeling that kept coming back: insecurity, and the sense that I had no talent.
authority.p2: I'd get lost on the fretboard and my improvising always sounded like an exercise — not like music.
authority.p3: I realized the problem wasn't technique, much less learning yet another scale. It was understanding how to train creativity to expand my vocabulary, and seeing that every solo needs structure. It doesn't matter if you play 5 notes or 50 — what matters is how you organize them so your solo actually sounds like a story.
authority.p4Strong: With strong visualization and an understanding of these structures, you no longer need inspiration or talent to create great solos.

heading.suffix: do you keep repeating the same phrases over and over?

1.title: Organization and a melody map
1.description: Creating melodies and phrases can't depend on having an inspired day. Whoever maps and organizes their visualization of scales unlocks new pathways and builds more options for their vocabulary.

2.title: Your technique is just a messenger
2.description: A solo with many or few notes doesn't really matter. Knowing how to amplify what you're truly good at and play the right notes — at the right time, with the right dynamics — is what sets your voice apart.

3.title: Every solo needs to sound like a story
3.description: Stories need structure, so a solo without structure just sounds like a pile of random notes. Whoever understands the structures can build and connect ideas with much more ease.

4.title: FEELING AND AWARENESS, TOGETHER
4.description: Building great solos comes down to how you structure them, expand your vocabulary, and understand elements like dynamics and interpretation to bring every new idea to life and make it unique.

heading.prefix: Learning inside Ciência das 6 Cordas is built on the

visualizacao.title: Visualization
visualizacao.description: Build a clear map of the fretboard for full freedom and fluency, with no limits. Standardize how you visualize scales, chords, and arpeggios.

criacao.title: Creation
criacao.description: Build your own language and understand solo structures in a conscious, creative way — without relying only on canned phrases. Step away from clichés and obvious patterns.

analise.title: Analysis
analise.description: Develop a self-critical ear so you can explore the same idea in many different ways, refine it, and repeat the process.

triade.demoLead: To build solos from scratch like these:
triade.footer: A logical learning sequence — far faster and more effective for building solos as good and authentic as the ones from your idols.

heading.prefix: Learn to create and unlock your

modules.subheading: Every concept and tool you need along the way to develop your musicality and make the way you play truly unique.

1.title: A solid foundation for solos
1.description: Build a unique, solid system for visualizing scales so that — beyond never getting lost on the fretboard again — you can see new pathways and melodic possibilities to kick off your creative process for phrases and solos.

2.title: Vocabulary building
2.description: Hands-on application of ideas so you can build your own phrases and pull existing phrases apart to apply them in real creation and improvisation contexts.

3.title: Building solos from ZERO
3.description: Develop the skill behind crafting solos — understanding the idea and structure from the simplest themes to the most complex ones, in a no-nonsense way.

4.title: Improvisation
4.description: How to bring all these creation tools into your improvisation naturally and sharpen your musical thinking.

5.title: Music Theory + Harmony
5.description: Music theory from zero to advanced, with a complete handbook and module full of practical examples — perfect for someone who has never studied any theory at all.

5.title: Updates

6.title: Extra materials

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subheading: Still have a question? See if the answer is right here.

1.question: Do I need previous guitar experience?
1.answer: Yes — the course is designed for guitar players who can already play at least the basics. You don't need to be advanced, but you do need to know the fundamentals of the instrument: how to position your fingers, play chords, and figure out songs.

2.question: How long does it take to see results?
2.answer: Results vary depending on how much you practice. The course is designed to deliver fast, progressive results.

3.question: Is access really for life?
3.answer: Yes. Once you buy, access is yours forever. No monthly fees, no renewals, no expiration date. Plus, all future updates are included.

4.question: Does it work for any musical style?
4.answer: The method is focused on developing creative structure, which applies to any style — but it leans more toward rock, blues, and fusion. The principles are universal, and you adapt them to your own musical context.

5.question: What if I don't like the course?
5.answer: You have a 7-day unconditional guarantee. If within that window you feel it's not for you, just request a refund and we return 100% of your money. No questions asked.

6.question: Are the lessons live or recorded?
6.answer: Lessons are recorded in high quality and available 24/7 on the platform. You learn at your own pace, rewatch as many times as you want, and move forward as your routine allows.

7.question: I want to learn techniques like alternate picking and so on — is this for me?
7.answer: No — the course is focused on people who want to start out in improvisation, or who feel stuck and want to develop their vocabulary. We don't teach the fundamentals of technique.

8.question: How does support work?
8.answer: You can ask questions directly on the platform or in the exclusive student community. Our team replies within 24 hours on business days.

finalCta.heading: Still have any doubts?
finalCta.subheading: Stop improvising on autopilot. Start making music with identity, freedom and personality — using the technique you already have today.
finalCta.guaranteeNote: 7-day unconditional guarantee — zero risk for you
